claims: Ferroptosis can be induced in cancer cells and is immunogenic both in vitro and in vivo.
Early, but not late, ferroptotic cells promote the phenotypic maturation of bone-marrow derived dendritic cells (BMDCs) and elicit a vaccination-like effect in immune-competent mice.
ATP and HMGB1 are passively released during ferroptosis and act as immunogenic signals associated with early ferroptotic cancer cells.
methods: coculture of ferroptotic cancer cells with BMDCs
summary: The study demonstrates that early-stage ferroptosis in cancer cells is immunogenic and can induce antitumor immunity, suggesting a new therapeutic strategy for cancer treatment.

title: Lymph protects metastasizing melanoma cells from ferroptosis
claims: Melanoma cells in lymph experience less oxidative stress and form more metastases than melanoma cells in blood.
Exposure to the lymphatic environment protects melanoma cells from ferroptosis, increasing their ability to survive during subsequent metastasis through the blood.
summary: The study reveals that melanoma cells in the lymphatic system are protected from ferroptosis, leading to increased metastasis through blood compared to those in the bloodstream.
